frame_analyses.description: Needle driver (left, black handle) approaching needle on pink foam pad. Needle positioned for grasping near mid-shaft.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle entry angle appears perpendicular to long axis; standard grip position at approximately 2/3 point from needle tip

frame_analyses.description: Needle secured in driver. Suture trailing from needle eye is visible. Preparing for first pass through Penrose drain.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle firmly grasped; suture aligned with needle trajectory

frame_analyses.description: Needle driver advancing toward first marked point on pink foam Penrose model. Needle entering at upper mark location.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Entry angle perpendicular to model surface; instrument alignment suggests accurate targeting of mark 1

frame_analyses.description: Needle passing through Penrose model from mark 1. Suture following needle path cleanly. Second pass being prepared.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean single-pass entry; no tissue drag or deviation observed

frame_analyses.description: Needle approaching second marked point on lower aspect of foam model. Entry at mark 2 location being established.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Needle positioned perpendicular to entry; mark 2 appears well-targeted with <1mm deviation

frame_analyses.description: Needle exiting Penrose model at mark 2. Both suture entry and exit complete. Suture loop forming around marked area.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean exit with no tearing or drag marks visible on foam

frame_analyses.description: Suture withdrawn from model. Both needle and suture ends now extracorporeal on white surgical pad. First knot throw being initiated (surgeon's knot - double throw).
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Right-hand driver forming first loop; suture ends clearly visible and separated

frame_analyses.description: First throw (surgeon's knot double throw) completed. Knot visibly formed with two wraps around standing line. Proper tension evident.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean double wrap with appropriate tightness; knot appears symmetric and well-formed

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher tool being loaded with first completed knot. Knot positioned at instrument opening for advancement toward model.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper knot pusher technique; knot secured in pusher without slipping

frame_analyses.description: Knot pusher advancing knot down suture toward model. First knot sliding smoothly along suture length with knot pusher maintaining control.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Smooth advancement; no evidence of knot slipping or loosening during push

frame_analyses.description: First knot arriving at model surface near entry point. Knot beginning to compress against Penrose drain surface.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Proper contact between knot and model; knot pusher maintaining support until knot fully seated

frame_analyses.description: Suture cut beyond the knot on model. Both tail ends trimmed with scissors. Task completion verified - knot secure on Penrose, suture ends cut.
frame_analyses.technique_notes: Clean cut without disrupting knot; appropriate cut length leaving small tail

penalties.description: Entry point at mark 1 approximately 1mm medial to marked line
Exit point at mark 2 approximately 0.5mm superior to marked line

knot_assessments.notes: Clean double wrap with symmetric tension; knot well-formed and held tightly during push

confidence_rationale: Completion time clearly visible from frame sequence. Suture deviations estimated from camera perspective on mark entry/exit points; angles make precise mm measurement uncertain. Knot formation and pusher technique well-observed. No visible drain compromise. Conservative penalty assignment due to minor measurement ambiguity from oblique camera angles.
cannot_determine: Exact mm deviation at mark 2 — camera angle is slightly oblique to exit point, making perpendicular measurement estimation approximate
Micromotion during knot push — resolution insufficient to detect sub-mm slippage
technique_summary: Excellent competent performance. Fast needle loading (8s, expert range). Suture placement accurate with minimal deviation (<2mm total). Clean surgeon's knot formation with proper double-throw syntax. Smooth knot pusher technique with secure final cinch. Completion time 210s places performance in upper-intermediate range. Penrose drain remained intact throughout with knot properly positioned at mark.
strengths: Rapid, efficient needle loading and grasping technique
Accurate suture passage through both marks with minimal deviation
Clean, well-formed surgeon's knot with proper tension
Excellent knot pusher control with no slippage during advancement
Secure final knot cinch with appropriate tail management
improvement_suggestions: Knot formation phase (70s) could be reduced to 50-60s with practice on hand coordination
Knot push phase (80s) is within acceptable range but could be optimized to 60-70s with more confident pusher technique
Overall time trajectory: aim for 160-180s completion with additional practice on extracorporeal knot tying efficiency
